GN[Some More Fun Before And After Winter Solstice, And Prolly Into 2024 (auto auto auto)]
C[Tom Newman: So I noticed in the previous games the variations didn't keep all the branches I played out. Is there something I need to do to keep them all?
Tom Newman: Also, this game shows no time limits (which is good), but I see a 3 day clock on whites info. Is that just to get started?
trohde: I have no idea, also noticed that … I _think_ there was a bug mentioned in the Forum
trohde: Oh, seems I omitted Time Limits …
Tom Newman: Ahh. Now it went away, good.
trohde: but that might be healthier anyway :-D
Tom Newman: We've been playing with no limits the last few games.
trohde: anyway, if I ever am away for too long you may always <ping> me and I’ll try to find a clever move if I’m still around :-)
Tom Newman: I'm never in a hurry or worried.
C[trohde: Oh, seems I omitted Time Limits …
Tom Newman: Ahh. Now it went away, good.
trohde: but that might be healthier anyway :-D
Tom Newman: We've been playing with no limits the last few games.
trohde: anyway, if I ever am away for too long you may always <ping> me and I’ll try to find a clever move if I’m still around :-)
Tom Newman: I'm never in a hurry or worried.
C[Tom Newman: Well, I guess I do worry, but only about how bad I play! 😂
C[trohde: Yeah, my worries too … well, not really worrying abt Go actually, but I honestly shake my head about my Go … I mean, falling back five stones is quite something
C[Tom Newman: Don't think of it that way. The world is just catching up to you!
C[trohde: Belatedly, a happy Solstice to you and yours!
Tom Newman: Hopefully Google got that right!
C[trohde: Thanks, my friend :-) That’s perfect (though we’d probably rather say “Frohe Sonnenwende”) but “glücklich” is wonderful!
C[trohde: Belatedly, a happy new year!
trohde: May it become your best so far!
C[Tom Newman: So far, I'm going backwards. Or everyone is catching up to me faster! I'm getting faster at losing. Is that a skill? Happy New Year!
trohde: And then? W doesn’t really have a plan
C[trohde: Yeah, same perceptions here re: speed and falling back
C[trohde: Tom. I MUST tell you. Jasper has died. I buried him on Saturday. Is why I switched to vacation.
Tom Newman: Oh no. Sorry for your loss.
C[trohde: I’m back, sort of, hopefully. Been in the basement of my emotional architecture for a while
Tom Newman: Glad to see you back at the board. Hope you are OK.
C[trohde: Ouf … I’m not really “OK”, TBH, but … shlepping myself along
C[Tom Newman: Are you thinking about getting a new friend?

C[trohde: _Thinking_ yes. Doing? No. At least not currently. I’m too much of a mess, healthwise and other.  Doesn’t feel responsible to do when I don’t know what I’m going to do, where I’m going to go, if at all I’m going somewhere.

trohde: Maybe this is the opportunity to prepare my departure to somewhere South. Still dreaming of my childhood home in South India, though I know it’s not the paradise anymore that it was at the time.

Tom Newman: Interesting to hear you say somewhere south is India. Very different southern living than here in the US. For our retirement activities, we purchased a camper van and trying to spend some time traveling.
C[trohde: Yeah, prolly to do with imaginations about the magical land where I spent my childhood … 🤷‍♂️
trohde: this game’s done, you kicked my butt :-D

C[Tom Newman: And so it begins again...

trohde: Good game, and good to see you across the board again!
C[Tom Newman: And so it begins again...
Tom Newman: So, did I understand your comment in the last game correctly - did you live in India?
C[Tom Newman: So I'm now 6 out of 21 vs you.  I don't think I'll catch up any time soon!
Tom Newman: We've now been playing together for 10 years. Wow.
C[trohde: Oh wow indeed 😊 ten years is a long time … I have only good things to say about you, not too many people “survive” so long with me, even virtually 😄

trohde: And game-wise … definitely feels like you’re catching up – or maybe, because it happens with others also … maybe it’s really me declining, or maybe just being that attentive (to think a little more positive 😉)
trohde: 1961–’67 in Kerala, South India, age 4–10
trohde: 1967–68 in Maharashtra, North India, age 10–11
trohde: my parents worked for German dev aid, father was an M.D., and mother was an MTA, both working and teaching in the hospital
trohde: parents and siblings stayed three more years, I had to go to Germany and live with foster parents for 3 years because parents believed that I should visit a German school 🙄
trohde: so … I’m actually a child of the Tropics … I spoke Malayalam fluently (like, THINKING in that language) but sadly lost all of it because I had to leave the environment before puberty
trohde: I wonder how much of my memory might actually be encoded (i.e. encrypted!) in Malayalam
trohde: would like to do some experiments with a native speaker interviewing me under hypnosis … but such a setup would be difficult to find 😄
trohde: I never really adapted to Germany — to the social climate, the mindset of “elbowing” oneself through and ahead of people … and also not with the meteorological climate

trohde: looking somewhat like the stereotypical German, blond and blue-eyed, I always felt somewhat alien inwardly, I mean, it feels like I sense things differently and I process them differently also.
trohde: There’s a name for this: Third Culture Kids (TCK), and there even exist forums etc., and even self-help groups
C[Tom Newman: Wow. That is so interesting. I bet you can pick up the language again. Would be very interesting experiment to see how quickly it comes back.
trohde: Above I meant to say: or maybe just NOT being that attentive

C[Tom Newman: I've never been to India, but did spend a year in Diego Garcia. Small island in the Indian Ocean. Literally the other side of the world and the middle of nowhere. Beautiful place. But the Brits had removed all the natives so no local population.
Tom Newman: I think anyone that travels gets a different perspective on the world. I wish more Americans had the opportunity, might change a lot of viewpoints.
C[trohde: Oh, only NOW I see that my greetings at the beginning have all gone to the MLog … I had set my prefs to default Game Chat to MLog 🙄
trohde: So here are the first four lines in this Game Chat:
trohde: Good game, and good to see you across the board again!
C[Tom Newman: I think this opening has been fairly balanced. But nervous for what's next. Not sure how black can get sente from white.
C[trohde: W still and again clueless
C[Tom Newman: Really worried about the large white moyo in the center. Desperate times, jumping in....
trohde: Well, then I’ll admit that I’m really worried about your corners and huge left side :-D
C[trohde: but leaving now for my bed :-)
Tom Newman: The public malkovich comments are fun! Good night.
C[Tom Newman: Here comes the invasion! (and more public commenting)
C[Tom Newman: Well that was a dumb move!
Tom Newman: My private comment was about how dumb my move was.
C[trohde: Well … me, I’m an expert dumb mover 😄
C[Tom Newman: So lesson learned, never admit how dumb your move was before you fully understand it. My go style seems like modern art - just slapping paint everywhere and sometimes it works out.
C[trohde: I guess it’s just crazy and useless …
trohde: esp. as I now can’t prevent B from playing T10
Tom Newman: I think the liberty at T13 keeps black alive in the corner.
Tom Newman: Also N19 is available if needed.
C[trohde: W resigning soon-ish, I guess
trohde: You kicked my ass again!
Tom Newman: 2 in a row is not much of a trend. Want to try again?

C[Tom Newman: So I just had someone make an intersting comment

C[trohde: Yup, it does happens, sadly.
trohde: And now, with a new incarnation of Katago that can play even more human-like, it is to be expected to happen more often.
trohde: IF you ever suspect this to happen, pls click the “call moderator” button
trohde: I for one am too weak a player though to even recognise it 🤷‍♂️
trohde: So, to keep my peace of mind, I simply assume that all my opponents are fair players :-D
Tom Newman: I'm in the NLI camp - natural lack of intelligence
C[Tom Newman: Just seems like having a computer play for you defeats the whole purpose of playing.
trohde: And yes, but ofc there exist people whose egos depend on artificial pampering
C[Tom Newman: Well personally, i don't mind a bit of pampering, just not from an AI! :-)
C[Tom Newman: So, turns out this is the Turing tournament. My playing partner was just letting me know that while it is allowed, he did not use any AI.

C[ygramul: This rengo thing works less smoothly than I thought 😅
C[Tom Newman: By less smooth do you mean your partner is dragging you down?  I feel like I'm way out of my league! Interesting for me and hope fully not too frustrating for everyone else.

Tom Newman: It's confusing to me to go back and see who played what moves.  When I try to catch up on the sequence since my last move I'm not sure who played what. Probably not that important to the current state of a game but something for feedback
Tom Newman: Does anoek every play rengo?
ygramul: Oh no no! I didn't mean it like that!

ygramul: What I was thinking is the issue that many people don't take the game very seriously and they just forget about the game and timeout. This cuts the rythm of the game, that doesn't really start until the only players that remain are the ones willing to play
ygramul: The issue that when it's your turn you have to review the last moves and maybe you won't understand their logic... I guess is part of the fun? 🤷‍♂️🤷‍♂️
C[Tom Newman: I agree it's interesting to come back to the game and catch up. It forces some more review and reconsideration.
Tom Newman: What is confusing is correlating moves to the players
Tom Newman: Maybe shorter times would help. Can you set up tournaments for this? Maybe something like bridge where 4 players play 3 games and each game with a different partner?
Tom Newman: Then the winner of the match is who has the most wins and they advance?
C[ygramul: I don't think you can set up rengo tournaments 🤔🤔
ygramul: At least I don't remember seeing the option, but I have not created that many tournaments
C[Tom Newman: I haven't seen it either. I'm sure @anoek could figure it out!
C[Tom Newman: Sorry to see this end by timeout.
Tom Newman: Happy to try again if you ever need an extra player.
ygramul: Thank you all for participating
bp7: Noooo, I'm so sorry, what a pity.

trohde: Also, forgive me: I’m in crisis mode currently, and have been for the past 31 days … nicotine withdrawal, and with horrible effects … been smoking for 51 years, which is very bad ofc … and had yet another attack of severe respiratory distress syndrome, no other path than to quit (ofc)
trohde: withdrawal effects: insomnia, mood swings (i.e. extreme increase of depression, up to being tired of living), concentration problems
